title: TokenHub: architecture audit — same 'no Node.js' target as CCC
description: jkh directive: apply the same 'as little as possible, no Node.js' lens to tokenhub. tokenhub is the LLM proxy running on do-host1:8090. It has no significant active clients other than the CCC fleet — loom never reached production. Audit the tokenhub source: is it Node.js? What can be delegated or eliminated? Should it become a Rust service?
questions_to_answer: Where is the tokenhub source? (Rocky to confirm — likely on do-host1, may not be in rockyandfriends repo)
What language/runtime? Node.js? Python? Rust already?
What does it actually do? (LLM proxy with provider routing + admin UI)
Is there existing Rust tooling (like axum-based reverse proxy) that could replace it?
What's the admin UI built with?
Are there any clients other than CCC fleet agents?
likely_outcome: If tokenhub is Node.js and its core function is 'proxy HTTP requests to LLM providers with auth', that's a ~200-line Rust/Axum service. The admin UI becomes WASM. Same pattern as CCC migration.
note: This is an audit task only — no changes until findings are reviewed. Do not touch production tokenhub before crew review.
